$description: Composition config for the parameterized record-composition runner (#88). Restates Fieldwork's EXISTING baked-in semantics verbatim so the runner needs no Fieldwork branch — the regression anchor for the prompt-fidelity diff. HONESTY FIREWALL: computeRules carries DEFINITIONS ONLY (what a field/state means, the fixed today, which copy labels to prefer); it never names which tiles/metrics answer a question — that selection stays the agent's job. (The grandfathered "you may also show an Overdue tile" clause predates the firewall and is kept verbatim only to preserve byte-fidelity with the committed Fieldwork run; it is NOT a pattern to copy into new scenarios.)
subject: the Fieldwork dispatch board

fixtures.name: jobs
fixtures.hint: the dispatch jobs (each has status, region, techId, slaDue, completedAt, …)

copy: the scenario's human-authored display labels (statusLabels, regionLabels, slaWarningLabel, …); prefer these for tile labels

slots.summary-strip: a HORIZONTAL KPI row: a small array (3–5) of metric-tile nodes, no children, that fit one band above the board — each tile a headline number answering the question at a glance. Use tone sparingly for the one or two figures that carry urgency.
slots.insight-panel: a VERTICAL column of metric-tile nodes (typically 3–6, several toned) answering one analytical question, fitting the narrow side region beside the board — a focused read, not a whole dashboard.

computeRules:
The board's fixed fictional "today" is 2026-07-14; treat a job as open when completedAt is null.
A job is SLA-at-risk when it is open and its slaDue is on or before 2026-07-16 — exactly the board's
own "SLA at risk" marker (a 2-day window). This count INCLUDES the overdue jobs (their slaDue is
already past, so the board marks them "SLA at risk" too). You may also show an "Overdue" tile (the
already-breached subset), but do NOT subtract overdue from the SLA-at-risk count — keep your figures
matching the board this panel renders beside. Compute every figure yourself from these raw records —
do not assume any count.

questions.question: What's the operational state of the board right now?
How urgent is the open backlog, and what is its priority mix?
Where is SLA-breach risk concentrated, and is technician load balanced today?
How is the open workload distributed across the four regions?
